title: Mobile login authentication failures
description: Multiple customers reporting inability to log in from mobile devices (iOS and Android). Error message: 'Authentication token validation failed'. Issue started after auth-service deployment on June 13.

impact.business_impact: Customer-facing login completely broken on mobile

timeline.event: Root cause identified: mobile JWT format mismatch
Mobile login restored for most users

root_cause: Auth-service deployment on June 13 changed JWT token format. Mobile apps were generating tokens in the old format, which the new validation logic rejected. Desktop web app was unaffected because it uses a different token flow.
resolution: Temporary fix: Accept both old and new JWT formats. Permanent fix needed: Update mobile SDKs to use new format and deprecate old format with a migration period.

title: Payment webhook processing failures
description: Razorpay payment webhooks are failing signature verification intermittently (approximately 5% failure rate). This causes payment status updates to be missed, resulting in orders showing as 'pending' when payments were actually successful.

impact.business_impact: Payment confirmations delayed, customer confusion, support tickets increasing

timeline.event: Sentry alerts for webhook signature failures spike
Redis connection errors also noticed in Sentry
Investigation narrowed to clock skew between servers
Identified intermittent Redis failures compounding issue

root_cause: Clock skew between TechFlow servers and Razorpay's timestamp-based signature. Additionally, intermittent Redis connection failures (ECONNREFUSED) are causing cache lookups for payment status to fail.
resolution: Pending. Planned fix: Increase timestamp tolerance window from 30s to 300s for webhook signatures. Fix Redis connection pooling configuration.

title: Database connection pool exhaustion
description: Production database connections leaking, causing pool exhaustion at 847/1000 connections. Multiple services affected with intermittent 503 errors. Connection count was climbing at 15 connections per minute.

impact.business_impact: All API endpoints degraded, intermittent 503 errors, customer-facing impact

timeline.event: Sneha begins investigation, identifies order-service connections not releasing
Deepak correlates with order-service v2.3.1 deployment 2 hours prior
Temporary fix: Increased pool limit to 1500, restarted order-service
Root cause confirmed: retry loop creating new connections without releasing failed ones
Staging monitoring: connections stable at 45-60 range for 20+ hours

root_cause: Order-service v2.3.1 introduced a bug in the database retry logic. When a query failed, the retry code created a new connection for each retry attempt without releasing the failed connection. Under normal load, this caused connections to leak at approximately 15/minute until pool exhaustion.
resolution: Fix applied in PR #133: Properly release connections in retry error handler. Add connection validation before use. Currently in staging monitoring — will deploy to production after 24-hour stability confirmation.

title: Email delivery rate degradation
description: Email delivery success rate dropped from 97% to 82% over the past week. Bounce handling appears broken — soft bounces are being retried indefinitely instead of being suppressed after 3 attempts.

impact.business_impact: Transactional emails (password resets, order confirmations) failing for ~18% of users

timeline.event: Notification service refactored and deployed (bounce handler changed)
Rate drops to 82%, PagerDuty alert triggered
Amit identifies soft bounce retry issue

root_cause: Notification service refactoring on June 13 accidentally removed the bounce counter check. Soft bounces are being retried indefinitely instead of being suppressed after 3 failed attempts. This results in repeated delivery to invalid addresses, which degrades sender reputation and delivery rates.
resolution: Pending. Need to restore bounce counter logic, add suppression list for repeatedly bounced addresses, and rebuild sender reputation.

title: Image processing service OOM kills
description: Image processing service has a memory leak causing OOM kills in production. Memory usage grows linearly until the 4GB limit is reached (~24 hours), then Kubernetes OOM killer terminates the pod. This has happened 3 times this week.

impact.business_impact: Image uploads fail intermittently when pods crash, user profile pictures and product images affected

timeline.event: First OOM kill observed (ignored as one-off)
Deepak requests production access for Sneha
Still blocked — production access not granted by DevOps

root_cause: Under investigation. Suspected: Image processing library (Pillow) not properly releasing memory after processing large images. Circular references preventing garbage collection. Need production profiling to confirm.
resolution: Blocked on production access. Temporary workaround: Pod restart every 12 hours via CronJob.

title: Kubernetes deployment failures - image pull errors
description: Kubernetes deployments intermittently failing in production. Pods hang in 'ContainerCreating' state and never start. Issue correlates with deployments to the new node pool added last week.

impact.business_impact: New deployments failing, unable to ship fixes and features reliably

timeline.event: Deployment of analytics-service fails — pods stuck in ContainerCreating
Manual retry succeeds on old node pool
Second deployment failure on new node pool
Root cause identified: registry auth not configured for new node pool
Fix deployed to staging, testing in progress

root_cause: New Kubernetes node pool added on June 14 did not have imagePullSecrets configured for the private container registry. Pods scheduled on old nodes worked fine, but pods on new nodes couldn't authenticate to pull container images.
resolution: PR #89 adds imagePullSecrets to all deployment manifests and configures proper registry authentication for the new node pool. Currently testing in staging.

title: API latency degradation - P95 at 1.2 seconds
description: API P95 latency increased from 200ms to 1.2s after payment-service v1.5.0 deployment. Affecting /api/v1/payments, /api/v1/users, and /api/v1/orders endpoints. Customer complaints received.

impact.business_impact: Slow page loads, increased error rates (2.3%), customer complaints

timeline.event: Root cause: missing indexes on transaction history queries
Additional cause: N+1 queries in orders endpoint
Fix deployed to staging, latency back to normal in staging

root_cause: Payment-service v1.5.0 added transaction history queries without proper database indexes. Additionally, the orders endpoint had N+1 queries loading related payment records individually instead of batch loading. Combined effect caused P95 latency spike from 200ms to 1.2s.
resolution: PR #220 adds missing indexes and optimizes N+1 queries. Staging shows P95 back to 180ms. Production deployment pending after staging validation.

title: Grafana dashboards showing stale metrics
description: After migrating to the new Prometheus cluster, several Grafana dashboards are showing stale or missing metrics. Data appears to stop updating after June 15 00:00 UTC.

impact.business_impact: Reduced observability — team cannot monitor real-time system health

timeline.event: Sneha notices stale metrics on API Response Times dashboard

root_cause: Grafana data source was not updated to point to the new Prometheus cluster endpoint. Old cluster still has data up to June 15 but no new data being scraped.
resolution: Need to update Grafana data source configuration to new Prometheus endpoint. Also need to verify all scrape configs are migrated.

title: CI pipeline failures on auth-service repository
description: CI build for auth-service has been failing since June 16. Error: ModuleNotFoundError for cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519. This is blocking all PRs on the auth-service repository, including the critical OAuth and XSS fix PRs.

impact.business_impact: All auth-service PRs blocked — cannot merge critical security and feature patches

timeline.event: Security dependency update triggers CI rebuild
CI build fails with cryptography module error
Vikram reports issue after noticing blocked PRs
Vikram offers to fix — incorrect version pin in requirements.txt

root_cause: Security dependency update pinned cryptography package to a version that removed the ed25519 submodule used by the auth-service JWT implementation.
resolution: Pending. Fix: Update cryptography version pin in requirements.txt to a compatible version that includes ed25519 support.

title: Search functionality returning incorrect results
description: Search feature returning incorrect results. Searching for 'invoice' returns 'inventory' results. Special characters in search terms cause empty results. Quotation marks in search cause 500 error.

impact.business_impact: Core search functionality degraded, customer complaints from multiple accounts

timeline.event: PROJ-1006 created with minimal description ('Search not working')
Customer support ticket #4521 with detailed reproduction steps
QA identifies possible correlation with input sanitization changes

root_cause: Under investigation. Possibly related to recent input sanitization changes that may be stripping or escaping search characters incorrectly.
resolution: Not yet started. Needs assignment and investigation.

title: AWS cost overrun - projected 23% over budget
description: AWS monthly spend projected to exceed budget by $3,500 (23%). Primary contributors are EC2 instances (+15%), RDS (+8%), and S3 (+22%).

impact.business_impact: Budget overrun, financial impact on Q2 numbers

timeline.event: Ravi submits Terraform PR #94 for cost optimization

root_cause: Combination of factors: over-provisioned EC2 instances, S3 storage growth without lifecycle policies, and unused reserved instances in wrong regions.
resolution: PR #94 submitted with optimizations: ARM-based instances, S3 intelligent tiering, RDS right-sizing. Estimated savings: $2,400/month.

title: Redis connection failures in payment service
description: Intermittent Redis connection refusals (ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:6379) in the payment service. 47 occurrences in the last hour affecting 12 users. Payment cache lookups failing, causing degraded performance and missed webhook updates.

impact.business_impact: Payment status lookups slow, some webhook updates missed

timeline.event: Sentry alerts spike for Redis ECONNREFUSED errors

root_cause: Redis connection pool exhaustion in payment-service. The service is not properly handling connection timeouts, leading to stale connections holding pool slots.
resolution: Pending. Need to configure connection pool health checks, add connection timeout settings, and implement retry with fresh connections.

title: SSL certificate expiration warning - 4 production domains
description: SSL/TLS certificates for 4 production domains expiring on June 20, 2026. If not renewed, all HTTPS traffic to these domains will fail, causing complete service outage.

impact.business_impact: If certificates expire: complete production outage for all web and API services

timeline.event: Ravi posts in #devops about certificate renewal

root_cause: No automated certificate renewal process in place. Certificate expiration monitoring relies on manual tracking.
resolution: Planned: Renew certificates during low-traffic maintenance window on June 19 at 10 PM IST. Post-incident: Set up Let's Encrypt auto-renewal.

title: Dependabot critical security vulnerabilities - admin panel
description: Dependabot flagged 3 critical security vulnerabilities in the admin panel frontend dependencies: webpack-dev-middleware path traversal (CVE-2024-29180), express open redirect (CVE-2024-29041), and follow-redirects authorization header leak (CVE-2024-28849).

impact.business_impact: Security risk — potential for path traversal, open redirect, and auth token leak

timeline.event: Dependabot creates GitHub issue #167 and email alert

root_cause: Dependencies not updated regularly. No automated dependency update process in place.
resolution: Pending. Rajesh assigned to patch all 3 vulnerabilities and verify nothing breaks.
